Sweet Hut Bakery & Cafe.note: Large seating, Wi-Fi, outlets — popular student spot.

Cafe Mozart Bakery.note: Renovated work area, natural light, free parking.

TwoHa's Cafe.note: Dividers for privacy, tons of seating, open till midnight.

White Windmill Bakery & Cafe.meetingRoomNote: Private booth-style rooms in the back, bookable on demand.

Land of a Thousand Hills Coffee.note: Connected to Thrive Coworking, built for work.

Alchemist on the Divide.note: Plenty of seating, but trendy and busy.

The Cream.note: Many outlets, but more brunch/dessert leaning.

Coffee That Matters.note: Large, quiet, built for remote work and study.

The Coffee By Hand.note: Wi-Fi, good weekday afternoons; livelier at night.

ChiCha San Chen.note: Crowded seating, better for short visits.

Paris Baguette.note: Lots of tables, Wi-Fi, commonly used for studying.

Tous Les Jours.note: Inside H Mart, seating exists but not focused on work.

Tree Story Bakery & Cafe.note: Quiet seating area for students and remote workers.

Vincent Bakery & Cafe.note: Ample seating with outlets, library-like atmosphere.

Hansel & Gretel Bakery Cafe.note: Spacious, charging spots, open late — can be noisy.

Georgia French Bakery & Cafe.note: Cozy local bakery; workable but not a study mecca.

Mai Bakery.note: Inside Great Wall food court, no real cafe space.

The Patio Cafe & Bakery.note: Plenty of indoor and patio seating.

Shokku Bakery.note: Inside Jusgo food court, counter service only.

Forest Cafe.note: Comfy sofa seating, some charging spots.

Cafe 104.note: Wi-Fi, outlets, open till 11pm — top-cited Duluth study spot.

Boba Mocha.note: Cozy with couches, indoor + outdoor, board games.

Orange Coffee Bar.note: Quiet, Wi-Fi, ample parking — recommended for working.

Tom N Toms Coffee Black.note: Huge 2-story cafe with upstairs study area.
Tom N Toms Coffee Black.meetingRoomNote: Private rooms upstairs — bookable via info@tomntomsatlanta.com

Kafeiin.note: Modern minimalist, Wi-Fi, comfy seating, open late.

Fujiissa.note: Counter inside Tokyo Kuma food court.

Yezi Coconut Dessert.note: Small dessert shop — not a sit-and-work spot.

Qamaria Yemeni Coffee Co..note: 3,600 sqft, ~140 seats, open till late — built for studying.

Yibna Cafe.note: Plenty of chairs and outdoor seating.

El Cafe by La Abuela.note: Dine-in works; not marketed for studying.

The Quynh Cafe.note: Vietnamese coffee/boba; on local quiet-study lists.

Lemon Sweets Bakery & Cafe.note: Cozy, fine for studying with friends.

Cafe Rothem.note: Outlets at nearly every seat, jazz, books — a study haven.
Cafe Rothem.meetingRoomNote: Conference room + private meeting room rentable hourly or daily.

Wich Tea.note: Plenty of seating, board games; takeout-leaning.

Hayat Coffee.note: 3,600 sqft, ~100-120 seats with study and lounge areas; open past midnight nightly.

Cafe Flat.note: Spacious clean design built for working and studying; locally roasted coffee.

Glaze Tea Duluth.note: Spacious, clean and inviting; kiosk ordering, dine-in friendly.

TwoHa's Cafe.when: Fri-Sat till 1am; rest of week till midnight

Hayat Coffee.when: Fri-Sat till 2am; rest of week till 1am
